channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NASA's Next-Generation Mars Helicopter: A Leap Forward in Space Exploration
item.description: NASA is developing a next-generation Mars helicopter that could revolutionize exploration of the red planet, featuring advanced navigation systems and larger payload capacity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 hidden reasons you keep running out of iCloud storage
item.description: Apple gives every iCloud user 5GB of free storage space. This storage space can be used for anything—from backing up your iPhone online to saving documents in the cloud to syncing photos across devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Vonage upgrades VCC Intelligent Workspace with AI tools to boost productivity
item.description: By integrating AI and APIs, Vonage’s platform enhances agent efficiency for better customer satisfaction.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Net neutrality never stood a chance
item.description: A federal appeals court sealed the fate of the Biden administration’s efforts to regulate internet providers on Thursday, striking down the Federal Communications Commission’s 2024 net neutrality order once and for all.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk turns Cybertruck explosion into a marketing moment
item.description: The new year started out with the worst kind of bang. In the early hours of January 1, an assailant exploded a Cybertruck in front of Trump Hotel in Las Vegas, using firework mortars and gas canisters.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why Apple TV+ is offering a free weekend of binge-watching
item.description: Apple TV+ is hoping people will make a dent in the Strategic Popcorn Reserve by bingeing its streaming TV and movies for free this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: VPNs are disappearing from India's app stores – and a 2022 law may be the culprit
item.description: The VPN removal orders appear to be the first enactment of the controversial 2022 India's data retention law. Here's what we know so far.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Apple settles Siri eavesdropping accusations for $95M
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the tech giant used its voice assistant Siri to obtain and share private conversations without users' permission.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: You can now play red light, green light in Call of Duty thanks to new Squid Game collab
item.description: Squid Game has arrived in Call of Duty, bringing free skins and new modes to help you get your squid on.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Latest Nvidia RTX 5000 power usage rumors make me scared that my PSU will be nowhere near enough for the RTX 5080
item.description: I’m likely going to have to stick with an RTX 5070 for my graphics card upgrade – or go AMD RDNA 4 instead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: PlayStation Classics port studio Implicit Conversions appears to be researching PS3 emulation
item.description: PlayStation Classics port studio Implicit Conversions appears to be researching PS3 emulation according to its roadmap.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: If your Google Nest smart speaker has been giving you the silent treatment, there's now a fix
item.description: Some users found that their devices stopped responding to voice commands between Christmas and New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft's Revolutionary Data Center Design: A Game-Changer for Sustainability
item.description: Microsoft unveils a new data center design that consumes zero water while offering liquid cooling, sparking hope for sustainable data center operations worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GOP lawmakers request Yellen briefing on Chinese hackers' Treasury breach
item.description: Republican lawmakers are swiftly urging the Treasury Department to provide answers on a recent cybersecurity attack from China that infiltrated various workstations, as outlined in a letter obtained by The Hill. “This breach of federal government information is extremely concerning.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Trump blamed the situation at the southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Seis formas de fracasar en la gestión del cambio
item.description: En la era de la transformación digital, el cambio organizativo es constante. Depende de los líderes de TI garantizar que los usuarios absorben y aplican los cambios que sus iniciativas digitales aportan a los flujos de trabajo empresariales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ser CIO en 2025: estos serán los principales desafíos tecnológicos
item.description: Anticipar cuáles serán las tendencias en tecnología para el próximo año tiene un poco de bola de cristal: su propio carácter disruptivo hace que en ocasiones entre en juego una herramienta que hasta entonces no había destacado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 10 hot programming trends
item.description: Tides ebb and flow. Pendulums swing. Ideas come and go, especially in programming, where the technologies and techniques for creating software continuously evolve. Some programming languages, libraries, platforms, and tools become hot and irresistible.
item.dc:creator: Info World
